description: Document the upstream t3code npx silent-failure investigation as durable harness knowledge: a topic memory note linking to the filed upstream issue (https://github.com/pingdotgg/t3code/issues/2621), a generalized 'silent npx install failure' troubleshooting recipe under docs/troubleshooting/, a one-line lesson in memory/MEMORY.md, and a CHANGELOG entry. Documentation-only; no application code; no fork/PR against the third-party repo.

userStories.title: Capture the investigation as a topic memory note
userStories.description: As an orchestrator agent picking up this thread later, I want a single source-of-truth note documenting the t3code triage so I do not re-derive the reproduction or root cause from npm logs.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at memory/t3-npx-silent-install.md
Note contains the verbatim reproduction command (npx --yes -p t3@0.0.22 t3 --help) with observed exit code and zero-byte stdout/stderr counts
Note contains the verbatim smoking-gun excerpt from Appendix A of prd.md (lines 1401-1416 of the npm debug log plus the gyp ERR! stack)
Note links to the upstream issue: https://github.com/pingdotgg/t3code/issues/2621
Note documents the proof that the CLI itself is reachable (npm install --ignore-scripts t3@0.0.22 + direct node node_modules/t3/dist/bin.mjs --help)
Note follows the topic-note conventions in context/rules/memory.md (placed under memory/<topic>.md, not under a daily-log subdir)
Pre-commit hook exits 0 when committing only this file

userStories.title: Add a generalized silent npx install failure troubleshooting recipe
userStories.description: As a future agent or contributor staring at a silently-failing npx <pkg>, I want a documented diagnostic recipe so I can identify and work around the failure in minutes instead of an hour.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at docs/troubleshooting/npx-silent-install-failure.md

Recipe is generic — covers the pattern (silent npx exit, native dep without prebuilds, install-hook stderr suppression), not just the t3code instance; t3code is referenced as the canonical example with a link to the upstream issue

Recipe documents Symptoms (exit 1 + zero-byte output), Diagnose (npm debug log location, scan latest *-debug-0.log for 'install { code: 1'), Confirm (install with --ignore-scripts then run the bin directly), Causes (no matching prebuild for platform/arch, missing build toolchain), Workarounds (install build tools, --ignore-scripts if dep is non-essential, file upstream issue requesting optionalDependencies), and Example (link to t3code investigation)
docs/troubleshooting/README.md exists (one-line directory-intent + bullet list of recipes per context/rules/directory-readme.md); links the new recipe

If docs/troubleshooting/ is genuinely new in git at commit time (git ls-files docs/troubleshooting/ returns empty pre-commit), a one-line annotation is added to docs/architecture/container-runtime.md under the ## Repo Layout {#repo-layout} heading per context/rules/repo-layout-source.md
Pre-commit hook exits 0 when committing only the US-002 files

userStories.title: Append a one-line lesson to memory/MEMORY.md
userStories.description: As the orchestrator running the qualify/improve loop after this session, I want the takeaway distilled to a single bullet under ## Lessons Learned so future sessions auto-load it without reading the full topic note.
acceptanceCriteria: Idempotency precheck: run grep -nE 'npm/_logs|npx swallow|silent.*npx' memory/MEMORY.md; if a semantically equivalent bullet exists, mark this AC satisfied without appending and document the existing line number in the commit message
If precheck returns no matches, append exactly one bullet to memory/MEMORY.md under the existing ## Lessons Learned heading

Bullet is one line, dated (YYYY-MM-DD), captures the lesson: 'When npx <pkg> exits silently with code 1, scan the npm debug log (default ~/.npm/_logs/, latest *-debug-0.log) for install { code: 1 — npm/npx swallow install-script stderr.'
Bullet links the topic note (memory/t3-npx-silent-install.md) and the upstream issue
No duplication with context/IDENTITY.md principles per context/rules/memory.md boundary
Append-only — no edits to existing entries

userStories.title: Add a CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased]
userStories.description: As a maintainer reviewing the next release, I want the new troubleshooting doc surfaced in the changelog per context/rules/git.md changelog policy.
acceptanceCriteria: Prerequisite check: harness tracking issue #263 exists (gh issue view 263 returns the tracking issue body)
One bullet appended under ## [Unreleased] → ### Added in CHANGELOG.md (create the ### Added subheading only if not already present under [Unreleased])

Bullet is imperative, mentions the new troubleshooting doc, links the tracking issue using the format ([#263](https://github.com/ryaneggz/open-harness/issues/263)) per context/rules/git.md § Changelog
No edits to versioned (## [X.Y.Z]) sections
No placeholder issue number — implementer must halt and surface a blocker if issue retrieval fails rather than guess
